ai_strategy_lead.name: Anupam Joshi
ai_strategy_lead.title: Vice Provost and Chief AI Officer

ai_strategy.summary: UMBC has not published a standalone university-wide AI strategy document, but has operationalized a clear AI posture through the 2024 establishment of the Center for Artificial Intelligence (CAI) and the July 2025 creation of a Vice Provost and Chief AI Officer role filled by Anupam Joshi. DoIT organizes AI initiatives across three pillars (Administrative, Instructional, Research) and pairs enterprise-licensed tools (Gemini, Copilot, Amplify) with faculty-development guidance rather than prescriptive rules. The Chief AI Officer is charged with aligning ongoing initiatives, guiding long-term planning, and leveraging university partnerships.

key_provisions: UMBC-authenticated Gemini, Copilot, and Amplify protect user data from training use
Only UMBC-verified tools are approved for FERPA/Level 1 data
AI detectors explicitly deemed unreliable and not licensed campus-wide
Syllabus guidance recommends listing permitted uses, prohibited uses, and citation expectations
Guidelines framed as faculty decision-support rather than prescriptive policy
ai_policy.summary: UMBC has published shared guidelines (not a mandatory policy) for AI use in the classroom and academic administration, developed through consultation between faculty and staff. The posture is permissive and framework-based: faculty retain discretion over classroom use, while DoIT steers the community toward UMBC-licensed tools with data-protection contracts for anything involving FERPA or institutional data.

bodies.name: Office of the Vice Provost and Chief AI Officer
bodies.scope: University-wide AI and computing strategy; alignment of academic, research, and administrative AI initiatives

bodies.name: UMBC Center for Artificial Intelligence (CAI)
bodies.scope: Interdisciplinary AI research, education, and application coordination across 50+ faculty and 25 labs

bodies.name: DoIT AI Initiatives program
bodies.scope: Enterprise AI tooling, faculty training, data protection, FERPA/Level 1 tool vetting

bodies.name: Faculty Development Center (FDC) AI Learning Communities
bodies.scope: Faculty learning communities on AI literacy and AI-enhanced teaching (AY 2024-25 cohorts)

bodies.name: Interdisciplinary Activities Advisory Committee (IAAC)
bodies.scope: Provost-level committee supporting interdisciplinary work including AI-adjacent research

governance_structure.summary: Governance is tiered: the Vice Provost/CAIO role (created July 2025) owns strategic direction; the Center for Artificial Intelligence (established 2024) coordinates research and education; DoIT operates enterprise AI tooling and training; and the Faculty Development Center runs faculty-facing AI literacy programming. There is no standalone senate-ratified AI council, but shared guidelines were produced through faculty-staff consultation.

initiatives.title: Appointment of Anupam Joshi as Vice Provost and Chief AI Officer
initiatives.description: In July 2025 UMBC created and filled a new Vice Provost and Chief AI Officer position. Anupam Joshi, a 30-year UMBC faculty member and director of the UMBC Cybersecurity Institute, was named to lead strategic direction for AI and computing, align ongoing initiatives, and guide long-term planning.
initiatives.significance: Signals R1-scale institutional commitment to AI governance by elevating AI leadership into the Provost's office rather than leaving it distributed across DoIT and colleges.

investment.description: Dedicated executive-level AI leadership position with responsibility also over academic resource planning and space management

initiatives.policy_implications: Creates single accountable executive for AI strategy spanning research, teaching, and administration.

initiatives.title: Establishment of the UMBC Center for Artificial Intelligence (CAI)
initiatives.description: UMBC established the Center for Artificial Intelligence in 2024 as an interdisciplinary hub consolidating four decades of AI research. The center spans 50+ faculty across 25 labs working on robotics, machine learning, NLU, data science, multi-agent systems, LLMs, knowledge graphs, and neural networks, and coordinates AI coursework across departments.
initiatives.significance: Provides a visible institutional front door for AI research and collaboration at an R1 whose AI work previously lived primarily inside departmental silos in CSEE and Information Systems.

investment.funding_source: UMBC / College of Engineering and Information Technology
investment.description: Physical home in ITE building, 50+ affiliated faculty, 25 labs

collaborations.description: Cross-unit and external research collaborations facilitated through the center

initiatives.title: Campus-wide deployment of Google Gemini and Microsoft Copilot (with data protection)
initiatives.description: UMBC expanded access in March 2025 so that all faculty, staff, and students receive UMBC-authenticated Google Gemini and Microsoft Copilot, with contractual data protections preventing use of inputs for model training. Free Gemini includes NotebookLM, Deep Research, and Google Gems.
initiatives.significance: Puts mainstream generative-AI access into every account with enterprise data-handling language — the R1 equivalent of utility-grade AI distribution.

investment.description: Enterprise licensing + contract terms that protect institutional data

collaborations.description: Enterprise AI contracts with data-protection terms

tools_and_vendors.name: Copilot
tools_and_vendors.role: Campus-wide generative AI assistant, integrated with M365 for Pro users

initiatives.policy_implications: Signals acceptable-tool list: UMBC-authenticated variants are cleared for FERPA/Level 1 data, unvetted public tools are not. Removes student paywall for frontier models, addressing tool-access equity.

initiatives.title: UMBC Amplify instance (Vanderbilt-developed GenAI platform)
initiatives.description: UMBC runs its own instance of Vanderbilt's Amplify platform, giving faculty and staff (by access request) a secure sandbox with commercial models including OpenAI GPT-4o and o1-series, Meta Llama, and Anthropic Claude, hosted on UMBC's cloud with contracts that prohibit training on university data.
initiatives.significance: Offers multi-model access for sensitive workloads without forcing UMBC to build its own LLM gateway — a scaled example of the inter-institutional Amplify consortium now spanning 50+ campuses.

investment.description: Employee-only secure multi-model sandbox via Amplify platform

collaborations.description: Access to commercial models via Amplify backend

tools_and_vendors.name: Amplify (UMBC instance)
tools_and_vendors.role: Secure multi-model LLM sandbox for employees

initiatives.policy_implications: Provides an audit-friendly, data-protected path to frontier models for staff workflows.

initiatives.title: myUMBC Answers AI-powered search and student-services chatbot
initiatives.description: In early 2025 the myUMBC team launched myUMBC Answers, an AI-driven search bar enrolled by default for all faculty, staff, and students. It answers questions about billing, financial aid, library services, career services, and IT support, and surfaces personal info such as grades, statements, and Retriever Card balances.
initiatives.significance: Moves UMBC from static portal search to LLM-assisted service discovery — representative of a broader R1 shift to embed AI in routine student-services touchpoints.

investment.description: Internal myUMBC team build, leveraging UMBC architecture

collaborations.description: Built in-house with DoIT myUMBC team; MS-IS student project contributed prototype work

tools_and_vendors.name: myUMBC Answers
tools_and_vendors.role: AI-powered portal search and Q&A assistant

initiatives.policy_implications: Includes explicit disclaimer that answers may be inaccurate and must be verified for consequential decisions.

initiatives.title: chip-gpu HPC expansion with NVIDIA H100 and L40S nodes for AI research
initiatives.description: UMBC's High Performance Computing Facility deployed 12 new GPU nodes (10 L40S and 2 H100) on the chip-gpu cluster, adding 320 CPU cores and 44 GPU cards across a 145-node cluster with 2+ PB storage. DoIT also runs a dedicated two-H100 node hosting Meta Llama for researchers needing on-prem LLMs.
initiatives.significance: On-prem H100-class capacity lets UMBC researchers train and fine-tune modern LLMs without cloud-grade spend, rare among mid-size R1s.

investment.description: 145-node chip cluster, 2+ PB Retriever Research Storage, dedicated LLM inference node

initiatives.title: ArtIAMAS cooperative agreement with U.S. Army Research Laboratory and UMD
initiatives.description: UMD, UMBC, and the U.S. Army Research Laboratory entered a five-year cooperative agreement worth up to $68 million to accelerate AI and autonomy in complex environments. UMBC contributes Information Systems expertise via PIs Aryya Gangopadhyay and Nirmalya Roy, working on shared simulation infrastructure, human-machine teaming, and AI-based networking and edge computing.
initiatives.significance: Anchors UMBC in federally funded frontier AI/autonomy research at nine-figure scale, and seeds the campus Center for Real-time Distributed Sensing and Autonomy (CARDS).

investment.description: Up to $68M over 5 years across UMD, UMBC, and ARL

collaborations.description: Multi-institution cooperative agreement on AI and autonomy

initiatives.title: iHARP — NSF Harnessing the Data Revolution Institute led by UMBC
initiatives.description: UMBC leads the NSF-funded Institute for Harnessing Data and Model Revolution in the Polar Regions, a five-year, $13 million HDR Institute using data science, machine learning, physics-informed ML, and causal AI to analyze climate and polar observations. Directed by Vandana Janeja, professor and chair of Information Systems.
initiatives.significance: Flagship AI-for-science institute demonstrating UMBC's ability to lead, not just participate in, national AI research consortia.

collaborations.description: Cross-university institute with UMBC as lead

initiatives.title: Graduate AI Certificate and AI/ML tracks in CS and Information Systems
initiatives.description: UMBC offers a Post-Baccalaureate Certificate in Artificial Intelligence (four-course/12-credit program through Information Systems, delivered online alongside master's students) plus an AI/Machine Learning track within the MS/PhD in Computer Science, and an AI & Knowledge Management research concentration in Information Systems.
initiatives.significance: Demonstrates credentialed AI pathways for working professionals and graduate students beyond one-off courses — the sector-standard move toward stackable AI credentials.

investment.description: Standing graduate programs in the Information Systems and CSEE departments

initiatives.title: Faculty Development Center AI learning communities and 'AI, Unscripted' podcast
initiatives.description: The FDC ran two AY 2024-25 AI faculty learning communities — 'Developing Students' AI Literacy Within and Across Disciplines' and 'Using AI to Enhance and Expedite Teaching' — along with workshops on LLM foundations and generative AI in teaching. The limited-run 'AI, Unscripted' podcast guides faculty 'from curiosity to confidence.'
initiatives.significance: Classic FDC-led faculty up-skilling model — the mainstream approach R1s are using to shift from policy-first to practice-first AI adoption.

investment.description: Faculty learning communities, podcast series, workshops

initiatives.policy_implications: Faculty discretion model reinforced by shared literacy rather than mandates.

initiatives.title: Annual UMBC AI Symposium
initiatives.description: Following the April 2024 'AI, Privacy, and Ethics Symposium,' UMBC hosts a 2025 AI Symposium titled 'Exploring the Gadgets & Gizmos in Your AI Toolkit,' a seven-week virtual Wednesday series (Oct 1–Nov 12, 2025) covering Gemini integration, Copilot, legal implications of generative AI, and discipline-specific applications.
initiatives.significance: Public signal that UMBC's AI posture is ongoing, not a one-off launch — and builds an institutional cadence around AI literacy.

investment.description: Annual series of virtual symposia on AI tools, policy, and ethics

initiatives.policy_implications: Includes legal-implications programming to orient staff to emerging compliance issues.

initiatives.title: Maryland Institute for Innovative Computing (MIIC) tech-talent pipeline
initiatives.description: UMBC administers MIIC through its Career Center, placing 178 interns with 8+ Maryland state agencies since 2022 — 34 paid tech/data/cyber/AI internships in summer 2024 through summer 2025 alone, backed by $805K+ cumulative state funding. CIO Jack Suess championed the initiative.
initiatives.significance: Pipeline evidence that UMBC's AI posture extends into workforce/government modernization — a model for state-sector AI literacy and applied talent development.

investment.description: $805K+ cumulative to UMBC-administered internship pipeline; 178 students placed since 2022

initiatives.title: UMBC Training Centers' Center for Applied AI (C4AI)
initiatives.description: UMBC's continuing-education arm, UMBC Training Centers, operates the Center for Applied AI (C4AI) in Columbia, MD. C4AI delivers half-day AI workshops, AI for Leaders and AI for Project Managers tracks, consulting, and community meetups to working professionals.
initiatives.significance: Non-credit adult-learning channel separate from degree programs — an increasingly standard R1 move to monetize and diffuse AI skills into the regional workforce.

investment.description: Dedicated Columbia, MD facility with curriculum, consulting, and events

initiatives.title: Blackboard AVA and AI Course Design Assistant for instructional use
initiatives.description: UMBC pointed faculty to Blackboard's AI Virtual Assistant (AVA) and AI Course Design Assistant — LMS-embedded tools for generating course structures, test questions, rubrics, assignment prompts, and student FAQ support.
initiatives.significance: LMS-embedded AI is becoming the fastest adoption route for cautious faculty, and UMBC's DoIT actively steers them toward it rather than leaving assessment redesign to each instructor.

investment.description: LMS-embedded AI features bundled with Blackboard contract

tools_and_vendors.name: AVA (AI Virtual Assistant)
tools_and_vendors.role: LMS-embedded student FAQ and feedback support

initiatives.policy_implications: Moves AI from ad-hoc faculty tools into supported LMS workflow.

initiatives.title: NSF Digital Twinning for Neurodegenerative Disease grant
initiatives.description: UMBC researchers received a nearly $900,000 NSF grant under the Foundations for Digital Twins as Catalyzers of Biomedical Technological Innovation (FDT-BioTech) program to develop AI-driven digital twins for studying neurodegenerative disease progression. Work continues through 2027.
initiatives.significance: Representative of AI-for-science investment flowing into UMBC's biomedical-AI pipeline, not only its traditional cyber/autonomy strengths.

computing_infrastructure.summary: UMBC's High Performance Computing Facility operates the 145-node 'chip' cluster with 2+ PB Retriever Research Storage. The chip-gpu partition spans 20+ nodes with NVIDIA RTX 2080Ti, RTX 6000, RTX 8000, L40S, and H100 GPUs — recently expanded with 10 L40S and 2 H100 nodes (44 GPU cards, 320 CPU cores added). DoIT also runs a dedicated two-H100 inference node hosting Meta Llama for on-prem LLM research, and maintains Azure/OpenAI, Google Cloud/Gemini, and AWS Bedrock contracts for cloud AI workloads.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Campus-wide free enterprise AI access
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Providing authenticated Gemini and Copilot to all students, faculty, and staff addresses student-level AI-tool equity by removing paywalls to mainstream generative AI.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: MIIC tech internships
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Paid state-funded internships targeting Maryland students (178 placements since 2022, avg GPA 3.59) create an AI-workforce on-ramp, including to historically underrepresented students given UMBC's minority-serving profile.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: UMBC-Shady Grove AI & Machine Learning virtual workshops
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Summer AI workshops at UMBC-Shady Grove extend AI access to regional, often non-traditional learners at a multi-institution campus site.

regulatory_context.summary: Maryland Governor Wes Moore signed Executive Order 01.01.2024.02 in January 2024 establishing seven principles for responsible AI in state government (fairness, equity, innovation, privacy, safety, transparency, accountability). The Artificial Intelligence Governance Act of 2024 (SB 818) was signed May 9, 2024 and took effect July 2024, creating a framework for state procurement and deployment of AI. The 2025 Maryland AI Enablement Strategy and Roadmap positioned 2025 as an 'experimentation and momentum' year. The University System of Maryland does not publish a system-wide AI policy, though principles of shared governance apply; UMBC's AI posture is therefore largely set at the institutional level.

narrative_summary:
The University of Maryland-Baltimore County (UMBC) is a mid-sized R1 public research university (13,530 total enrollment in Fall 2025) in the University System of Maryland whose AI posture has moved from decades of faculty-driven research depth into a newly formalized institutional program. UMBC's AI credibility rests on forty years of research — more than 50 affiliated AI faculty across 25 labs — consolidated in 2024 into a single Center for Artificial Intelligence. In July 2025 UMBC created a Vice Provost and Chief AI Officer role filled by Anupam Joshi, a long-time faculty member and director of the Cybersecurity Institute, elevating AI leadership into the Provost's office with explicit responsibility for strategy, long-term planning, and resource alignment.

On the enterprise side, DoIT (led by long-serving CIO Jack Suess, who announced retirement in February 2025 pending a successor) has built a three-pronged stack: Google Gemini and Microsoft Copilot for every campus account, a UMBC-hosted Amplify instance (Vanderbilt open-source platform) giving staff secure access to GPT-4o, Claude, and Llama, and in-house tools like myUMBC Answers — an AI chatbot embedded in the student portal since early 2025 — plus Blackboard's AVA and AI Course Design Assistant. DoIT also operates the chip-gpu HPC cluster, recently expanded with NVIDIA H100 and L40S nodes and a dedicated on-prem Llama inference node.

Policy-wise UMBC is permissive-but-framed: shared faculty-staff guidelines rather than mandatory rules, explicit guidance that AI detectors are unreliable, UMBC-vetted tools approved for FERPA/Level 1 data, and faculty discretion over syllabus-level rules. Research flagship commitments include the NSF HDR iHARP Institute ($13M, polar AI), the ArtIAMAS cooperative agreement with UMD and the U.S. Army Research Laboratory (up to $68M, AI and autonomy), and a $900K NSF FDT-BioTech digital-twinning award. Workforce impact flows through the UMBC-administered Maryland Institute for Innovative Computing pipeline (178 student placements, $805K+ state funding) and UMBC Training Centers' Center for Applied AI in Columbia. Remaining gaps: no published standalone AI strategy document, no explicit senate-ratified AI council, and uncertainty during the CIO transition.

gaps_acknowledged: No published standalone UMBC AI strategy document could be located; strategy is inferred from operational materials and leadership appointments
Formal adoption date of UMBC's AI guidelines/framework is not published publicly
Current CIO successor has not been named as of the most recent sources (Jack Suess remains in role pending search)
No publicly disclosed user counts for campus-wide Gemini/Copilot/Amplify deployments
Exact gift/donor-funded AI investments (beyond federal grants) are not disclosed online
No reported controversies or litigation specifically tied to UMBC AI use were identified
